channel.title: Wahaj Ayub - Research Radar
channel.description: Selected papers in topological photovoltaics, quantum geometry, noncentrosymmetric materials, Hopf phases, and mathematical condensed matter.

item.title: The bulk photovoltaic effect in one-dimensional tellurium nanowires for self-powered broadband polarization-sensitive photodetection
item.description: A single tellurium nanowire produces a zero-bias broadband photovoltaic response and polarization-sensitive detection from 405 to 1550 nm.

item.title: Monopole Spin Density Wave States in Magnetic Weyl Semimetals
item.description: Monopole spin-density-wave order in magnetic Weyl semimetals carries a pairing Berry phase, protected gap nodes, and ideal quantum geometry in the weak-coupling limit.

item.title: Rotation topological states: theory and material realization
item.description: Decomposing occupied bands by rotation eigenvalue reveals hidden Z2^n topology and surface double-Weyl points that conventional occupied-space invariants miss.

item.title: ZPAN: An Organic Nonlinear Optical Crystal for High Intensity THz Generation
item.description: Noncentrosymmetric ZPAN crystals combine scalable growth, a polar axis, and strong optical rectification to generate broadband terahertz fields near 1 MV/cm.

item.title: Magnetic quadrupole current generation and accumulation in noncentrosymmetric systems
item.description: Symmetry permits magnetic-quadrupole currents broadly in noncentrosymmetric point groups and predicts measurable edge accumulation in noncentrosymmetric antiferromagnets.

item.title: Emergent induction in magnetic Weyl semimetals
item.description: Magnetization dynamics in magnetic Weyl semimetals produces emergent induction with relaxation-time-dependent intraband and Weyl-node-separation-dependent interband contributions.
